summary: Implement global keyboard listeners for Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V to handle object duplication with a spatial offset in the DocumentContext.

plan.title: Analyze existing clipboard state and actions
plan.description: Examine `client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx` to verify `clipboardObject` state and ensure `COPY_OBJECT` and `PASTE_OBJECT` actions are correctly implemented in the reducer.

plan.title: Implement keyboard shortcut listener
plan.description: Add a global `keydown` event listener in `client/src/components/editor/Canvas.tsx` (or a dedicated hook) to intercept Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V, triggering the respective dispatch actions.

plan.title: Refine Paste Logic with Offset
plan.description: Ensure the `PASTE_OBJECT` logic in the reducer or a helper function applies a visual offset (e.g., +20px X and Y) so duplicated objects don't perfectly overlap, and generates a new unique ID.

acceptance_criteria.description: Pressing Ctrl+C when an object is selected updates the `clipboardObject` in `DocumentState`.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Verify `editorReducer` handles `COPY_OBJECT` by setting `state.clipboardObject` to the object matching `uiState.selectedObjectId`.

acceptance_criteria.description: Pressing Ctrl+V creates a new object in the `objects` array with a unique UUID.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Verify `PASTE_OBJECT` action in `editorReducer` uses `uuidv4()` for the new object's ID.

acceptance_criteria.description: Pasted objects are placed with a coordinate offset relative to the original.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Check that `PASTE_OBJECT` logic increments `x` and `y` properties of the pasted object by a fixed value (e.g., 10 or 20).

acceptance_criteria.description: Keyboard listeners are cleaned up on component unmount.
Paste operation is blocked if the clipboard is empty or no layer is active.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Verify conditional check in `PASTE_OBJECT` reducer case for `state.clipboardObject` existence.

risks: Conflict with browser-native copy/paste if not using `e.preventDefault()` correctly for selected text.
Race conditions between state updates if rapid-fire pasting occurs.
Paste might fail if the original layer of the copied object was deleted.
